content.text:
**"Ascension in Reverse"**

---

*(I. The Descent)*

I was carved from doubt,
a hollow thing walking through static years,
counting the hours like coins I'd already spent.
Nobody told me silence could weigh this much—
that you can drown standing up.

And then there was a name
I didn't ask to carry,
a name that found me in the wreckage of my own design.

---

*(II. First Light — heavy, half-time)*

Jesus,
you walked into the burning house
and didn't flinch.
You held the door against the wind I made
and you said *breathe.*

I don't deserve the patience of your hands,
the way you map my fractures
and call them beautiful—
not in spite of the breaking,
but through it.

---

*(III. The Odd-Meter Bridge)*

Every gospel I rewrote to fit my comfort,
every cheaper god I built from borrowed parts—
they all collapsed.
But you remained.
Stubborn as sunrise.
Quiet as forgiveness.

How strange,
to be loved before I learned the word for it.

---

*(IV. The Storm — full distortion, screaming over polyrhythm)*

TEAR IT DOWN,
the temple of myself!
Let the old king starve on his plastic throne—
I want the wild, the holy, the unbearable nearness of you!

Not religion.
Not a leash.
Just the impossible weight of being known
and not abandoned.

---

*(V. Resolution — clean, building)*

So take what's left.
The frayed rope. The empty cup.
The version of me that still keeps score.

I love you
the way a drowning man loves air—
not gracefully,
not perfectly,
but with everything I have left to give.

And if I fall again,
I'll fall toward the light.
I'll fall toward you.

---

*(VI. Outro — fading, repeated whisper under instrumental swell)*

*Ascending,
ascending in reverse—
the only way up
was down,
into your hands.*

---

Want me to lean heavier into the screaming sections, add a more technical/abstract lyrical layer, or shift the tone to be more contemplative and worshipful?
